response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map GIS work, and has demonstrated distributed systems competence at Sociomantic handling high-throughput data pipelines. However, the core technical requirement for this role — modern C++ or Rust — is effectively absent: his only C/C++ work dates to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and Rust is not mentioned anywhere. His current and recent stack is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js. There is also no evidence of experience with routing algorithms, tiling formats, navigation guidance systems, or production map data pipelines, which are central to this role.
strengths: Geospatial domain experience: GISCollective open-source geo-data platform and Green Map GIS application demonstrate familiarity with GIS concepts and tooling
Distributed/high-performance systems background: Sociomantic role involved microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency requirements
Broad technical range and willingness to acquire new skills across stacks and paradigms, evidenced by mobile, embedded, and backend work across many languages
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience — the only C/C++ work is over a decade old (NI LabWindows, 2014-2015) and unrelated to systems or geospatial pipelines
No experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tiling, or production navigation guidance systems, which are the core subject-matter requirements
Primary expertise is in web/application development (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB), which is misaligned with the Linux systems and compiled-language focus this role demands
